title: Family obligation pressure
situation: A relative says you should agree because family always comes first.
sample_phrase: Real family would do this without asking so many questions.
mirror_questions: Can I love my family and still think clearly?
Is belonging being made conditional on automatic agreement?
What would care look like with consent included?
gentle_response: I care about this family. I still need room to think and choose clearly.

title: Faith and fear pressure
situation: Someone frames honest questions as proof that your faith or character is weak.
sample_phrase: A real believer would not question this.
mirror_questions: Can sincere faith include honest questions?
Is this encouraging humility and truth, or fear of rejection?
Do I feel free to seek understanding?
gentle_response: My questions are part of seeking truth carefully. I want this conversation to leave room for conscience and humility.

title: Workplace family language
situation: A workplace uses family language to ask for unsustainable sacrifice.
sample_phrase: We are a family here, so everyone needs to sacrifice.
mirror_questions: Are clear expectations being replaced with emotional language?
Would this request still seem fair without the family framing?
What boundary would make this sustainable?
gentle_response: I care about the team, and I also need clear expectations and sustainable boundaries.

title: Hidden enemy hook
situation: A post uses a vague enemy and special knowledge to pull you into fear or isolation.
sample_phrase: They do not want you to know this. Everyone else is asleep.
mirror_questions: Is this making me clearer, kinder, and more grounded?
What evidence can be checked outside this source?
Is this expanding my world or narrowing it?
gentle_response: I am willing to examine claims, but I need evidence, humility, and grounding.

title: Deadline sales pressure
situation: A seller pushes a quick decision before you can compare or think.
sample_phrase: This price is only available if you decide today.
mirror_questions: Would this still make sense if I had time to compare options?
What information am I being rushed past?
gentle_response: I do not make important decisions under deadline pressure. I will step back and compare options first.

title: Love as a test
situation: Someone makes your love or care feel dependent on saying yes.
sample_phrase: If you loved me, you would not need boundaries.
mirror_questions: Does healthy love make room for boundaries?
Do I feel free to say no and still be cared for?
What would love look like with consent included?
gentle_response: I love you, and boundaries help love stay healthy. I will not give up my boundaries to prove care.
